title: SQL Injection: Login Bypass
objective: Bypass the login form on the victim's internal admin panel.
briefing: Recon shows an admin panel at http://{ip}/admin/login. The login form posts username and password to /admin/login. No rate limiting or WAF detected.
hints: Try submitting something unusual in the username field.
SQL comments can end a query early: --
debrief: The login form built a SQL query by pasting your username straight into it, something like: SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '<what you typed>' AND password = '...'. Normally that checks a real username. But by typing ' OR '1'='1' -- you closed the quote early, added a condition that's always true (1=1), and used -- to comment out the rest of the query (including the password check). The database ran: SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '' OR '1'='1' -- ... which matches every row, so the server let you in as the first user it found, without ever knowing a real password. This is SQL Injection (OWASP A03:2021) — the fix is to never build queries by pasting user input into them; use parameterized queries/prepared statements so user input is always treated as data, never as part of the SQL command.

logs_append: [AUTH] admin login from {ip} - bypassed via malformed query
[AUTH] failed login attempt for user '{user}' from {ip}
